name: Brother INKvestment Tank MFC-J4335DW
evidenceBasis: Published Brother U.S. product, supplies, and Refresh subscription documentation. Not independently tested.
summary: A color inkjet all-in-one that uses Brother LC406 cartridges and offers USB, Wi-Fi, and Wi-Fi Direct. Brother markets it as Refresh subscription-ready, but also sells ordinary LC406 cartridges for the model.
bestFor: A home office that needs color, scanning, copying, fax, automatic two-sided printing, and a documented path to buy ordinary cartridges instead of joining a print plan.
notFor: Buyers who want refill bottles, Ethernet, a manufacturer-documented Linux driver, or proof that third-party cartridges will keep working after future firmware changes.
pros: Brother documents standard and high-yield LC406 replacement cartridges outside the Refresh program.
USB, Wi-Fi Direct, AirPrint, and Mopria provide several documented printing paths.
Automatic duplex printing and print/copy/scan/fax functions are documented.
limitations: The official U.S. specification page lists Windows and macOS drivers, not Linux.
Refresh subscription cartridges may stop working after cancellation; Brother tells users to have non-Refresh cartridges ready.
No official source reviewed promises support for third-party ink or protection from future firmware restrictions.
noLockInRelation: The relevant advantage is choice between documented retail LC406 cartridges and an optional Refresh plan—not freedom from a proprietary cartridge family. Local USB and Wi-Fi Direct reduce cloud dependence, but we did not verify first-time setup without an account.
dataBoundary: Manufacturer documentation confirms interfaces, functions, consumables, warranty, and subscription behavior. We did not test setup, cancellation, firmware, print quality, yield, reliability, or non-Brother supplies.

specs.label: Print technology / color
specs.value: Color inkjet

specs.label: Functions
specs.value: Print, copy, scan, and fax; color and black-and-white

specs.label: Automatic duplex
specs.value: Yes, default; up to Letter size

specs.label: Connections
specs.value: Hi-Speed USB 2.0, 802.11 b/g/n Wi-Fi, Wi-Fi Direct

specs.label: Mobile printing
specs.value: AirPrint, Mopria, Brother Mobile Connect, Wi-Fi Direct

specs.label: Consumables
specs.value: Brother LC406 standard or high-yield cartridges; separate black, cyan, magenta, and yellow

specs.label: Subscription
specs.value: Refresh EZ Print is available by enrollment and can be cancelled; subscription cartridges may deactivate after cancellation, so non-Refresh cartridges are required to continue

specs.label: Non-subscription use
specs.value: Standard LC406 cartridges are sold for this model; continued use without Refresh is documented through non-Refresh cartridges

specs.label: Official OS listing
specs.value: Windows and macOS on the U.S. product page; Linux is not listed

specs.label: Warranty
specs.value: 2-year limited warranty listed for the U.S. product

ownershipChecks.label: Subscription choice
ownershipChecks.note: Enrollment and cancellation are documented; ordinary cartridges are sold separately.

ownershipChecks.label: Basic use without subscription
ownershipChecks.note: Brother directs cancelled subscribers to replace Refresh cartridges with non-Refresh cartridges.

ownershipChecks.note: LC406 standard and high-yield families are identified.

ownershipChecks.note: USB and Wi-Fi Direct are listed.

ownershipChecks.note: Product and support material exists, but future firmware and third-party supply behavior are not promised.

ownershipChecks.note: Core U.S. specifications are detailed; Linux support and account-free initial setup are not established.

name: Epson EcoTank ET-2850
evidenceBasis: Published Epson U.S. support documentation and ET-2850 user guide. Not independently tested.
summary: A color inkjet all-in-one with refillable tanks, automatic two-sided printing, USB, Wi-Fi, Wi-Fi Direct, and a 100-sheet rear feeder.
bestFor: A household that wants refill bottles instead of cartridges and can live with a rear paper feeder, flatbed scanner, and wireless rather than Ethernet networking.
notFor: Buyers who need an automatic document feeder, Ethernet, a manufacturer-listed Linux package, or a guarantee about third-party ink and future firmware behavior.
pros: The standard ET-2850 uses user-filled ink tanks rather than replaceable cartridges.
USB and Wi-Fi Direct provide documented local connection options.
Automatic two-sided printing, AirPrint, and a 100-sheet plain-paper capacity are documented.
limitations: The scanner is flatbed-only and there is no automatic document feeder.
The reviewed U.S. user guide lists Windows and macOS requirements but does not list Linux.
ET-2850 and subscription-only ET-2850U are different products; marketplace listings must be checked carefully.
noLockInRelation: The standard ET-2850 has a documented bottle-fill workflow and local interfaces. That is materially different from Epson ReadyPrint models ending in “U,” which Epson says require an active subscription. It still relies on Epson's tank design, software, and policies, and we found no official promise covering third-party ink.
dataBoundary: The reviewed sources document the standard U.S. ET-2850, not ET-2850U. We did not test account-free setup, ink compatibility, print quality, maintenance, firmware, yield, or reliability.

specs.label: Print technology / color
specs.value: Color inkjet with refillable ink tanks

specs.label: Functions
specs.value: Print, copy, and flatbed scan

specs.label: Automatic duplex
specs.value: Yes for supported paper types and sizes

specs.label: Connections
specs.value: Hi-Speed USB, 802.11 b/g/n Wi-Fi, Wi-Fi Direct

specs.label: Mobile / remote printing
specs.value: AirPrint plus optional Epson Connect cloud services; Wi-Fi Direct is also documented

specs.label: Paper capacity
specs.value: Up to 100 sheets of Letter/A4 plain paper in the rear feeder

specs.label: Consumables
specs.value: Bottle-filled black, cyan, magenta, and yellow ink tanks

specs.label: Subscription
specs.value: No subscription requirement is stated for the standard ET-2850; Epson identifies models ending in “U,” including ET-2850U, as ReadyPrint subscription devices

specs.label: Official OS listing
specs.value: Windows and macOS in the reviewed user guide; Linux is not listed

specs.label: Warranty
specs.value: Epson publishes separate U.S. warranty statements based on purchase date; consult the current statement on the support page

ownershipChecks.note: Epson distinguishes the retail ET-2850 from subscription-only U-series hardware.

ownershipChecks.note: The standard model guide documents bottle filling and local printing without describing a plan requirement.

ownershipChecks.note: Core hardware and network behavior are covered; Linux and account-free first setup are not established.

name: Canon MegaTank PIXMA G3270
evidenceBasis: Published Canon U.S. product specifications and support documentation. Not independently tested.
summary: A color MegaTank all-in-one with refillable GI-21 bottles, USB, dual-band Wi-Fi, Wireless Direct, a flatbed scanner, and a 100-sheet rear tray.
bestFor: A home user who values refillable tanks, straightforward print/copy/scan functions, dual-band Wi-Fi, and Wireless Direct more than speed or automatic duplexing.
notFor: Buyers who need automatic two-sided printing, an automatic document feeder, Ethernet, or official proof of unrestricted third-party ink use.
pros: Canon identifies the GI-21 bottle family and fill volumes in its U.S. specifications.
USB, 2.4/5 GHz Wi-Fi, and Wireless Direct are documented.
AirPrint, Mopria, Canon PRINT, and Chromebook compatibility are listed.
limitations: Document printing is simplex only; automatic duplex is not listed.
Scanning is single-sided flatbed only, with no automatic document feeder.
Canon labels the printer ARS-capable, but this does not prove the printer is free of every account, app, or platform dependency.
noLockInRelation: Refillable GI-21 bottles and local USB/Wireless Direct paths reduce reliance on cartridge subscriptions and cloud printing. The printer remains tied to a specific ink system and Canon software ecosystem; no reviewed official source guarantees third-party ink compatibility or permanent policy behavior.
dataBoundary: Canon's U.S. specifications support the listed hardware facts. We did not test setup, ARS enrollment, account requirements, ink compatibility, firmware, print quality, yield, or reliability.

specs.label: Print technology / color
specs.value: Color inkjet with four-color continuous ink supply system

specs.label: Functions
specs.value: Print, copy, and single-sided flatbed scan

specs.label: Print speed
specs.value: Approx. 11.0 ipm black and 6.0 ipm color, Letter simplex ESAT

specs.label: Automatic duplex
specs.value: No; document printing capability is listed as simplex

specs.label: Connections
specs.value: Hi-Speed USB, 2.4/5 GHz Wi-Fi, Wireless Direct

specs.label: Mobile printing
specs.value: AirPrint, Mopria, Canon PRINT, Canon Print Service

specs.label: Paper capacity
specs.value: 100 sheets of plain paper in rear tray

specs.label: Consumables
specs.value: GI-21 pigment black plus GI-21 cyan, magenta, and yellow dye ink bottles

specs.label: Subscription / replenishment
specs.value: Automatic Replenishment System capable; the reviewed page does not state that ARS is required

specs.label: Official OS / protocol listing
specs.value: Windows, macOS, iOS/iPadOS, Android, and Chromebook; AirPrint and Mopria

specs.label: Warranty
specs.value: 1-year limited warranty with InstantExchange Program in the U.S.

ownershipChecks.label: Subscription choice
ownershipChecks.note: ARS capability is listed, but the product page does not fully explain the non-ARS setup boundary.

ownershipChecks.label: Basic use without subscription
ownershipChecks.note: The specifications document ordinary GI-21 bottles and local interfaces, but we did not find an explicit account-free setup promise.

ownershipChecks.label: Consumable transparency
ownershipChecks.note: GI-21 colors, ink types, and fill volumes are specified.

ownershipChecks.label: Local connection
ownershipChecks.note: USB and Wireless Direct are listed.

ownershipChecks.note: Canon maintains specifications, downloads, manuals, and warranty links on one support page.

ownershipChecks.label: Documentation completeness
ownershipChecks.note: Hardware details are strong; Linux and account-free first setup are not established.

name: Brother HL-L2460DW
evidenceBasis: Published Brother U.S. product specifications and support documentation. Not independently tested.
summary: A monochrome laser printer with automatic duplex, Ethernet, USB, dual-band Wi-Fi, Wi-Fi Direct, a 250-sheet tray, and official Windows, macOS, Linux, and ChromeOS compatibility listings.
bestFor: A text-heavy home office that wants wired or local-network printing, automatic duplex, Linux listed by the manufacturer, and no need for color, scanning, or copying.
notFor: Anyone who needs color, scanning, copying, fax, or evidence that third-party toner will remain accepted after every firmware update.
pros: Brother explicitly lists Linux compatibility and common local network protocols including IPP/IPPS and LPR/LPD.
Ethernet, USB, dual-band Wi-Fi, and Wi-Fi Direct offer several local connection paths.
Automatic duplex, a 250-sheet tray, and separate toner and drum supplies are documented.
limitations: It prints black and white only and has no scanner or copier.
Toner and drum are proprietary Brother supply families.
No reviewed official source promises third-party toner compatibility or unchanged firmware behavior.
noLockInRelation: The strongest ownership case is documented Linux support, Ethernet/USB/local protocols, and ordinary TN830-family toner plus DR830 drum supplies. Refresh is available on eligible Brother hardware, but subscription cartridges have separate cancellation rules; this page does not treat subscription eligibility as freedom from supply lock-in.
dataBoundary: Manufacturer documents hardware, protocols, OS compatibility, supplies, and warranty. We did not test driverless discovery, distribution-specific scanning (there is no scanner), firmware, third-party toner, page yield, print quality, or reliability.

specs.label: Print technology / color
specs.value: Electrophotographic monochrome laser

specs.label: Functions
specs.value: Print only

specs.label: Print speed
specs.value: Up to 36 ppm black

specs.label: Automatic duplex
specs.value: Yes, enabled by default

specs.label: Connections
specs.value: Hi-Speed USB 2.0, Ethernet, 2.4/5 GHz Wi-Fi, Wi-Fi Direct

specs.label: Mobile / local protocols
specs.value: AirPrint, Mopria, Brother Mobile Connect, IPP/IPPS, LPR/LPD, mDNS, and Port 9100 among listed protocols

specs.label: Paper capacity
specs.value: 250-sheet standard tray plus single-sheet manual feed

specs.label: Consumables
specs.value: TN830, TN830XL, or TN830V toner; DR830 drum

specs.label: Subscription
specs.value: Brother Refresh is a separate enrollment service; cancellation may require replacing Refresh cartridges with non-Refresh supplies

specs.label: Official OS listing
specs.value: Windows, macOS, Linux, and ChromeOS

specs.label: Warranty
specs.value: 1-year limited warranty in the U.S.

ownershipChecks.label: Subscription choice
ownershipChecks.note: Refresh requires enrollment; ordinary toner families are separately documented.

ownershipChecks.label: Basic use without subscription
ownershipChecks.note: The product ships with a starter toner and documents ordinary TN830-family replacements.

ownershipChecks.label: Consumable transparency
ownershipChecks.note: Toner and separate drum part families are identified.

ownershipChecks.label: Local connection
ownershipChecks.note: USB, Ethernet, Wi-Fi Direct, IPP/IPPS, and LPR/LPD are listed.

ownershipChecks.label: Long-term support material
ownershipChecks.note: Product, protocol, driver, and supply documents exist; third-party toner and future firmware behavior are not promised.

ownershipChecks.note: Core hardware, protocols, Linux compatibility, supplies, and warranty are all covered by official U.S. sources.
